text: Talmudic study culture was organized around the chavruta — the learning pair — precisely because the rabbis understood that attention sustains itself through accountability. When you study alone, the drift toward distraction meets no resistance; when you study with a partner, your wandering mind becomes visible as absence. The chavruta practice transformed attention from a solitary discipline into a relational obligation. Rabbi Yochanan Ben Zakkai, who preserved rabbinic learning after the Temple's destruction, reportedly said that a student without a study partner "has no Torah." The implication is not about content but about the quality of focus that dialogue alone can produce.
question: According to the passage, what did the chavruta system primarily accomplish?

options.text: It allowed scholars to cover twice as much material in the same time
It transformed attention from a solitary discipline into a relational obligation
It ensured that disputed interpretations could be quickly resolved
It preserved oral traditions that could not be written down
